Q5 MCQ Not checked
General Aptitude • ELECTRICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Astronomer: Most stars are born in groups of thousands, each star in a group forming from the same parent cloud of gas. Each cloud has a unique homogeneous chemical composition. Therefore whenever two stars have the same chemical composition as each other, they must have originated from the same cloud of gas.
Which of the following, if true, would most strengthen the astronomer’s argument?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
It is given that each cloud has a unique homogeneous chemical composition and the stars originated from the same cloud of gas have the same chemical composition. Thus, it can be concluded that whenever a star forms, it inherits the chemical composition of its parent cloud of gas. Hence, option (c) is correct.

Q6 MCQ Not checked
General Aptitude • ELECTRICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

People tend to estimate the likelihood of an event’s occurrence according to its salience; that is, according to how strongly and how often it comes to their attention. By placement and headlines, newspapers emphasize stories about local crime over stories about crime elsewhere and about many other major events.
It can be concluded on the basis of the statements above that, if they are true, which of the following is most probably also true?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
We know that newspapers’ coverage of local crimes is stronger than that of non-local crimes.
Nothing is said about frequency of coverage so, we can not assume anything about it. However, all else being equal, we can assume that people tend to think the local crimes outnumber non-local crimes.

∴ Only Q is necessary.

Q17 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science & Artificial Intelligence • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
S1 : Feature extraction can help improve model performance when the original features are noisy or highly correlated.
S2 : Feature selection reduces overfitting, improves model interpretability, and decreases computational cost.
Which of the above statements is correct?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
• Feature extraction (e.g., PCA) transforms correlated or noisy features into new uncorrelated features, often improving model performance. So, S1 is correct.
• Feature selection chooses a subset of original features, which reduces overfitting, improves
interpretability, and lowers computational cost. So, S2 is correct.

Q19 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science & Artificial Intelligence • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Consider two relations R1(A, B, C) and R2(A, B, C) with the same schema. If these relations are combined using set union, which of the following statements is true regarding functional dependencies?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
• Set Union combines tuples from relations with the same schema.
• However, it does not enforce functional dependencies that depend on relationships across
tuples from different relations.
• Therefore, some FDs may be lost after union, even if the schema matches.

Which of the following conditions is NOT a requirement for A* search to be complete (i.e., guaranteed to find a solution if one exists)?

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
Admissibility is required for optimality, not completeness. A* is complete even with a nonadmissible
heuristic, provided the other conditions are met.

Q24 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science & Artificial Intelligence • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
S1 : A model with High Bias is often characterized by being underfit to the training data, meaning it is too simple to capture the underlying relationship.
S2 : Stochastic Gradient Descent (SGD) uses the gradient calculated from all the training examples in the dataset to perform a single weight update.
Which of the above statements is correct?

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
• A high-bias model is too simple to capture the true relationship in the data. It fails to fit the training data well, resulting in underfitting. So, S1 is correct.
• Stochastic Gradient Descent (SGD) updates weights using only one or a few examples at a
time. Using all training examples is Batch Gradient Descent, not SGD. So, S2 is incorrect.

Q26 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science & Artificial Intelligence • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Which of the following is true about linked list implementation of queue?
S1 : In enqueue operation. If new node is inserted at beginning of linked list, then in dequeue operation must be removed from end.
S2 : In enqueue operation. If new node is inserted at the end, then in dequeue operation node must be removed from the beginning.

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
To keep the first in first out order a queue can be implemented using linked list in 2 ways.
1. If a new node is inserted at the beginning of linked list, then in dequeue operation node must
be removed from end.
2. If new node is inserted at the end of linked list then, in dequeue operation, node must be
removed from beginning.

Q28 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science & Artificial Intelligence • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

The term ‘slow mixing’ in Gibbs Sampling refers to a high degree of autocorrelation between successive samples. This typically occurs when:

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
Slow mixing in Gibbs Sampling occurs when the variables in the joint distribution are highly
correlated with each other. In such cases, updating one variable at a time results in very small
movements in the state space, causing successive samples to be very similar. This creates high
autocorrelation and makes the Markov chain explore the distribution very slowly.
Therefore, strong inter-variable correlation is the main reason for slow mixing.

Which of the following operations, commonly associated with the OLAP analytical environment, involves reducing the dimensionality of the data by aggregating or summarizing values along a dimension hierarchy

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
Roll-Up (or consolidation) climbs up the dimension hierarchy, aggregating data across lower levels to present a higher-level summary, such as combining sales from individual stores into regional sales.

Q36 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science & Artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following schema:
Department (dept_ID, dept_name, dept_Location)
Employee (emp_ID, emp_Name, emp_DeptID, emp_salary)
emp_DeptID is a foreign key referring to dept_ID of the department relation. Assume that each
department has atleast one employee.
What does the following query return?

 
Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
• Inner sub query identifies all the employees in a particular department (emp_DeptID) whose
salary is less than 50000. It retrieve emp_ID for those employee where the emp_DepID match
the dept_ID from the outer query.
• Outer query select the dept_name from the department relation. The condition is based on the
NOT EXISTS clause, which means the outer query will only return department for which the
inner query finds no employees with a salary less than 50,000. So, all employee earn 50,000.
So, option (a) is correct.

Q38 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science & Artificial Intelligence • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

A machine learning model is trained on a dataset. It achieves an almost zero training error, but when evaluated on a test dataset, the error is extremely high. This behavior suggests that the model fits the training data very well but fails to generalize to unseen data.
Which of the following options best describes the state of this model?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
Since the model achieves almost zero training error, it fits the training data very well, indicating
low bias. However, the extremely high test error shows that it fails to generalize to unseen data,
which is a clear sign of high variance. This behavior is typical of an overfitted model.

This w is also known as the:

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
The computational complexity of exact inference algorithms like Variable Elimination is exponential
in the treewidth of the network, which corresponds to the size of the largest clique formed
during the elimination process.

Q52 MSQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science & Artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
(a) A be skew-symmetric matrix of odd order, then determinant is zero.
(b) Maximum number of distinct eigen values of matrix A < size of matrix.
(c) For all 3 × 3 matrices A and B, AB = BA
(d) If you have an m × m matrix A that is invertible then, the column of A form a basis for Rm.
Which of the above statement is/are correct?

Correct answer: A, D
Solution

(a, d)
• Skew symmetric matrix is odd order then determinant is always zero. So, option (a) is correct.
• Maximum number of distinct eigen values = size of matrix A. So, option (b) is incorrect.
• Matrix multiplication is not commutative thus, AB ≠ BA. So, option (c) is incorrect.
• Am × m which is invertible thus |A| ≠ 0 i.e., linear combinations of columns space of A covers
the whole Rm. So, option (d) is correct.

Q56 MSQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science & Artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: A, C
Solution

(a, c)
• A back edge in DFS goes from a node to its ancestor. If a graph has a cycle, DFS produces a
back edge. A DAG has no cycles, so DFS on a DAG cannot have back edges. So, option (a) is
correct.
• In BFS for undirected graphs, all non-tree edges connect either:
(i) current level → previous level → considered a back edge
(ii) current level → same level → considered a cross edge
So BFS may have back edges in undirected graphs, and calling them “cross edges only” is not
correct. So, option (b) is incorrect.
• Bellman-Ford works for graphs with negative edges as long as there is no negative cycle. It
iteratively relaxes edges to compute correct shortest distances. So, option (c) is correct.
• Prim’s algorithm works fine even with negative edge weights, because it only picks the
minimum weight edge at each step to grow the MST. Negative edges do not affect correctness.
So, option (d) is incorrect.

Q60 NAT Not checked
Technical • Data Science & Artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0
Not attempted

A queue Q supports the usual operations enqueue(x) to add an element and dequeue( ) to remove
and return an element.
Consider the following Python code segment:

 
What will be the value of the variable count when the above code finishes execution?
NAT
Correct answer: 12
Solution

12
The queue starts with the value 1, and in every iteration the dequeued value x generates two new
values: 2* x and 4* x.
As the queue processes numbers in increasing order, the sequence of dequeued values becomes:
1, 2, 4, 4, 8, 8, 16, 8, 16, 16, 32.
The 11th dequeue gives x = 32, so the loop stops at that moment.
Since count starts at 1 and increases once per iteration, the final value becomes 12.

Q61 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science & Artificial Intelligence • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

If all edge costs in a graph are identical and equal to 1, how does UCS behave?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
When every edge in the graph has the same cost (cost = 1), UCS expands nodes in order of
increasing path cost, which basically becomes the same as exploring nodes level by level. That’s
exactly how BFS works. So with uniform edge costs, UCS behaves just like BFS, because both end
up expanding the shallowest unvisited nodes first
